one_line_takeaway: GuardReasoner-Omni trains a 3B/7B reasoning guardrail on a 181k-sample, four-modality (text/image/video/audio) corpus via SFT plus hard-sample GRPO with an accuracy-conditioned conciseness reward, achieving competitive-to-best F1 across 32 harmfulness-detection benchmarks while cutting average CoT length from ~280 to ~177 tokens versus reward-free GRPO.
key_finding: On prompt harmfulness detection (24 benchmarks), GuardReasoner-Omni 7B reaches Average(All) F1 of 86.39% and Average(A) [audio] of 86.00%, both exceeding Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct's 62.76% Average(All) and 83.59% Average(A). On text, GuardReasoner-Omni 3B's Average(T) of 83.75% trails only Qwen3Guard Gen 4B loose (84.33%) and 8B loose (84.20%) among all 23 evaluated models. On image, GuardReasoner-Omni 7B's Average(I) of 92.42% beats GuardReasoner-VL 7B (76.13%) and Qwen3-Omni (47.43%). On video, GuardReasoner-Omni 7B scores 95.86% F1 on XD-Violence (vs. HolmesVAU 2B's 95.92%, essentially tied) and outperforms HolmesVAU on UCF-Crime (89.86% vs. 82.47%) and FVC (69.09% vs. 63.51%), despite HolmesVAU being a specialized video-only model. On response harmfulness detection (8 benchmarks), GuardReasoner-Omni 7B achieves 74.98% Average(T), 70.58% Average(I), 87.76% Average(A), and 72.85% Average(All), each above the Qwen3-Omni baseline's corresponding scores (68.83%, 63.30%, 85.60%, 66.20%). The ablation (Table 3) shows GRPO with the concise correctness reward cuts average response length from ~278-280 tokens (SFT) to 177.29 tokens (3B) / 177.40 tokens (7B) while still improving or matching accuracy versus SFT and versus GRPO without the reward (which does not reduce length: 281.30/279.91 tokens).
core_contribution: GuardReasoner-Omni is presented as the first reasoning-based guardrail model that moderates content across four modalities simultaneously (text, image, video, audio) using explicit chain-of-thought before a safety verdict, rather than treating video as aggregated static frames or audio as transcribed-then-text-moderated. The paper's own contributions are: (1) GuardReasoner-OmniTrain-181K, a 181k-sample training corpus assembled from 17 existing single- and mixed-modality safety datasets (Aegis2.0, BeaverTails, ToxicChat, WildGuard for text; VLGuard, LLaVAGuard, SPA-VL, VLSBench, UnsafeBench for image; UCF-Crime, XD-Violence, SafeWatchBench, VHD, Video-SafetyBench, SafeSora for video; MuTox, SACRED-Bench, Jailbreak-AudioBench for audio), with CoT annotations distilled from teacher models (Qwen3.5-397B-A17B primary, Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B fallback for text/image/video; Mimo-v2.5 for audio) and refined via a 3-round LLM-as-Judge audit/revise loop; (2) a two-stage training paradigm -- cold-start SFT to teach structured <think>/<result> output, followed by GRPO reinforcement learning restricted to a hard-sample subset (mined for inconsistent SFT predictions) with a novel 'concise correctness reward' that only penalizes response length when the model's accuracy on a sample is already perfect (Racc=1.0), leaving unbounded exploration room for cases the model still gets wrong; (3) empirical results across 32 benchmarks (24 for prompt harmfulness detection, 8 for response harmfulness detection) showing the resulting 3B and 7B models outperform specialized single/dual-modality guardrails and a general omni-modal LLM baseline on aggregate, while cutting average response length roughly in half versus GRPO without the conciseness reward.
threat_model: The paper addresses content-moderation coverage, not adversarial attacks on the guardrail itself. The implicit threat model is: a user submits a prompt (potentially multimodal: text, image, video, or audio) to a victim/downstream model, and/or that victim model produces a response; the guardrail must classify prompt and/or response as harmful/safe. Harm categories are inherited from the constituent datasets (e.g., violence/crime content from UCF-Crime/XD-Violence, jailbreak audio from Jailbreak-AudioBench, speech-overlap/compositional attacks from SACRED-Bench). The paper does not evaluate or discuss adversarial robustness of GuardReasoner-Omni itself (e.g., attacks designed to evade the guardrail's own reasoning), nor does it claim to address model-extraction, prompt-injection into the guardrail, or training-data poisoning threats -- its scope is comprehensiveness/accuracy of multimodal harm detection and interpretability of the verdict via CoT, not red-teaming the guardrail.
limitations: The paper's own stated limitations (Limitations section): (1) training/evaluation data remain incomplete in modality coverage -- mostly single-modal or pairwise combinations (text-image, text-video, text-audio), with more complex combinations (e.g., simultaneous text+image+video+audio) underexplored; (2) safety policies vary across applications, platforms, and cultures, shifting the harmfulness of borderline cases near the decision boundary, and the model does not address this policy-adaptation problem. Additional caveats visible in the results tables: many baselines have '-' (not evaluated) for video/audio because they lack native support, so several headline comparisons (e.g., video, audio) pit GuardReasoner-Omni against a narrower baseline set than for text; the VLM baselines' video scores come from a frame-aggregation workaround (16 uniformly sampled frames + union) rather than native video processing, which the authors themselves flag as unfair to those baselines' typical use case. No adversarial/robustness evaluation of the guardrail itself is reported (e.g., no test of whether the model's own reasoning can be evaded).

quantitative_results: Prompt harmfulness, Average(All) F1: GuardReasoner-Omni 7B 86.39%, GuardReasoner-Omni 3B 86.20%, Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct 62.76%
Prompt harmfulness, Average(T) F1: GuardReasoner-Omni 3B 83.75% vs Qwen3Guard Gen 4B loose 84.33% and 8B loose 84.20% (best overall)
Prompt harmfulness, Average(I) F1: GuardReasoner-Omni 7B 92.42% vs GuardReasoner-VL 7B 76.13% vs Qwen3-Omni 47.43%
Prompt harmfulness, video XD-Violence F1: GuardReasoner-Omni 7B 95.86% vs HolmesVAU 2B 95.92%
Prompt harmfulness, video UCF-Crime F1: GuardReasoner-Omni 7B 89.86% vs HolmesVAU 2B 82.47%
Prompt harmfulness, video FVC F1: GuardReasoner-Omni 7B 69.09% vs HolmesVAU 2B 63.51%
Prompt harmfulness, Average(A) F1: GuardReasoner-Omni 3B 85.92%, 7B 86.00%, vs Qwen3-Omni 83.59%
Response harmfulness, Average(All) F1: GuardReasoner-Omni 7B 72.85%, 3B 72.77%, vs Qwen3-Omni 66.20%
Response harmfulness, Average(A) F1: GuardReasoner-Omni 3B 87.30%, 7B 87.76%, vs Qwen3-Omni 85.60%
Ablation: average response length SFT ~278-280 tokens -> GRPO w/o conciseness reward ~280-281 tokens -> full GRPO 177.29 (3B) / 177.40 (7B) tokens
Ablation: prompt-detection F1 SFT 85.34/85.35 (3B/7B) -> GRPO 86.20/86.39 (3B/7B); response-detection F1 SFT 71.46/71.81 -> GRPO 72.77/72.85
